notes: STRENGTH 12-card gravity deck. The carnival lot in October working on Roy: small Lila-shaped absences, the carousel's quarter-degree turn, the wind moving objects that should not move. Effects bias toward STRAIN gain unless Roy LET GO or STAND GROUND this turn.

cards.title: THE DOOR CREAKS IN NO WIND
cards.flavor: The cage door moves a half-degree on its hinge. The flag on the big-top doesn't move. The October air is the kind of still that you can hear.

then.text: You hold the door at the half-degree. The creak stops because you stopped letting it move.

cards.title: THE CAROUSEL TURNS A DEGREE
cards.flavor: You're not facing it when it does it. You hear it. The horses' poles brush their housings the way you remember from the year Lila spooked at the merry-go-round.

effects.text: A degree. You count to ten. You don't turn around.

cards.title: THE SMELL OF LION COMES BACK
cards.flavor: Not strong. The half of it that survives the lot's three months. The half that's in the wagon's wood.

cards.title: YOU SAY HER NAME WITHOUT MEANING TO
cards.flavor: Out loud. Not loud. The lot hears it the way the lot used to hear you say it in May.

then.text: You say it. The wagon registers the saying.

cards.title: THE BANNER POST LEANS WEST
cards.flavor: It was straight last Tuesday. Tonight it's at a degree. The post will fall in February. The post will fall whether you watch it or not.

cards.title: THE KID AT THE FENCE
cards.flavor: Miss Lavergne's youngest. Six years old. Watches the lot through the chain link. He has been here every afternoon for a week without saying anything.

cards.title: THE OLD DOG IN THE GRASS
cards.flavor: From the field behind the big-top. Three-legged. Watching the cage. Not approaching. Not leaving.

cards.title: THE AUCTIONEER'S TRUCK
cards.flavor: Diesel, coming up the highway from the south. You hear it before you see it. He told you Tuesday. It's Tuesday.

cards.title: YOU CARRY THE DOOR WITHOUT MEANING TO
cards.flavor: You put your hand on the bar. The door is the kind of weight you don't think about. You think about it.

then.text: You set the door's weight back. The hinge takes it again. That is what hinges are for.

cards.title: THE SUN GETS LOW
cards.flavor: Brass into copper into the colour the lot was when you first parked here in '88.

effects.text: Hour later than you thought. October is short.

cards.title: OLD PELL THROUGH THE BIG-TOP
cards.flavor: Across the field, under the half-down canvas. He's walking with both canes tonight. He came to see you.

cards.title: YOU HAVE NOT LET GO
cards.flavor: Three months. October light. You are standing where you have been standing every afternoon. The cage door is at the angle it has been at since the day Lila stopped fitting through it.

options.label: Put the bolt-cutters on the door (cost: 2 Time; gain: −3 STRAIN)

effects.text: The hinge gives. The door comes off in two cuts. You set it on the dirt.
